zotero.abstractNote: Dataset condensation aims to compress large datasets into a small set of synthetic samples that retain the performance of models trained on the original data. While existing methods focus on training efficiency, we identify a critical limitation: they often fail to capture the diversity of the original data, leading to poor evaluation performance on unseen test sets. We introduce DISCO (Diversifying Sample Condensation), a novel framework that explicitly optimizes for diversity in the condensed set. By leveraging a determinantal point process (DPP) objective, DISCO encourages the selection of samples that cover the feature space more effectively. Our experiments demonstrate that DISCO significantly outperforms state-of-the-art condensation methods across various benchmarks, particularly in scenarios where model evaluation requires high fidelity to the original data distribution.
